calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-30-2020 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-14-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: William Levy appeared by telephone for Annette Bendow.

Mr. Levy confirmed that the insurance policy is being paid out over time, that once the entire amount is paid out the policy will then be concluded and that he believes it was not paid out in a lump sum for tax purposes. The Court thanked Mr. Levy for providing the supplement. The Court granted the Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-29-2020 Continued To Jul-13-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Byron Levy appeared by telephone for Annette Benbow (Petitioner).

The Court stated that there is the question of the annuity which is payable over time and that it needs to be valued by the Probate Referee. Mr. Levy stated that there is some confusion, that the annuity does not provide for payment over time, that there are two annuities and that they are both payable on death in lump sums. Mr. Levy also stated that the sole heir elected not to take the payment last year, that the heir has up to five years to take the lump sum and that there are no instalments. The Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account is continued to July 13, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Levy is to file a declaration as to that effect along with an annuity statement showing that the annuity is to be paid in one lump sum.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jun-29-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-03-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
William Levy for Petitioner

Mr. Levy spoke of decedent signing a new will in 2018 thinking he lost the 2015 will, then destroyed the 2018 will after finding the 2015 will, and presented a new proposed order.

The Court granted the petition for Probate of Will and Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $20,000.00 is required. Status on close of estate is set for December 30, 2020.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: C. Wu. Not reported.
